response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE OR ENVIRONMENT. IT IS USED TO DESCRIBE HOW HOT OR COLD A PLACE OR OBJECT FEELS. IN SCIENCE, TEMPERATURE IS MEASURED USING DIFFERENT SCALES SUCH AS CELSIUS, FAHRENHEIT, AND KELVIN. THE UNIT OF TEMPERATURE IN THE INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM OF UNITS IS THE KELVIN. SCIENTISTS STUDY TEMPERATURE TO UNDERSTAND CLIMATE, WEATHER, AND PHYSICAL CHANGES IN MATTER. THE HIGHEST TEMPERATURE EVER RECORDED ON EARTH WAS IN LIBYA IN 1994. THE LOWEST TEMPERATURE EVER RECORDED ON EARTH WAS IN ANTARCTICA IN 1983. TEMPERATURE CAN AFFECT THE SPEED OF CHEMICAL REACTIONS AND THE BEHAVIOR OF MATERIALS. IT IS ALSO IMPORTANT IN MEDICINE TO MONITOR BODY TEMPERATURE. THE BODY MAINTAINS A RELATIVELY CONSTANT TEMPERATURE THROUGH METABOLIC PROCESSES. THE ROLL OF TEMPERATURE IN DAILY LIFE IS EXTREMELY SIGNIFICANT. THE ROLL OF TEMPERATURE IN DAILY LIFE IS EXTREMELY SIGNIFICANT. THE ROLL OF TEMPERATURE IN DAILY LIFE IS EXTREMELY SIGNIFICANT.
